channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Two House Dems join GOP to ban biological males from girls' school sports
item.description: The House of Representatives has passed legislation that would restrict girls' school sports to only those children who were born female at birth, excluding transgender kids.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hochul's tax cut plan: What it means for New Yorkers
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to cut taxes for more than 8.3 million taxpayers in New York. The proposal was announced as part of her 2025 State of the State address.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11

item.title: Biden administration will announce it's lifting state sponsor of terrorism designation for Cuba
item.description: The Biden administration is expected to lift the U.S. designation of Cuba as a state sponsor of terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Aamer Madhani, Colleen Long And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he will create an 'External Revenue Service' agency to collect tariff income
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday announced plans to create a new agency called the External Revenue Service to collect tariffs and other revenues from foreign nations.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson orders US Capitol flags raised to full height for Trump's inauguration
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson has ordered that flags at the U.S. Capitol be raised to their full height on Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration, 11 days after missing Carter funeral
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama will not attend the inauguration of President-elect Trump on Monday, the second time in two weeks that she will be absent from a significant event.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 ways the debt limit fight could play out under Trump
item.description: President-elect Trump and congressional Republicans are plotting how to address the nation’s debt limit this year. The debt ceiling, which caps how much money the Treasury can owe to pay the country’s bills, was reestablished earlier this month after being suspended for the past year and half.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson backs conditions on California fire aid
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is backing placing conditions on disaster assistance for California in the wake of the destructive wildfires blazing around the Los Angeles area, a position already drawing opposition from Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Final chapter of the special counsel's Jan. 6 case against Trump is now public. Here's what to know
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Less than a week before President-elect Donald Trump is sworn into office, a report from special counsel Jack Smith is refocusing attention on the brazen steps he took to cling to power at the conclusion of his first term.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What Americans think about Pete Hegseth, Trump's Defense Secretary pick
item.description: Pete Hegseth faces an important test on Tuesday — but he won’t just have to prove himself to the senators who immediately began sparring over his nomination as Defense Secretary.
item.dc:creator: Linley Sanders, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge: DFL candidate's election stands for Shakopee area Minnesota House seat
item.description: State Rep. Brad Tabke, a DFL Minnesota House incumbent who narrowly won reelection, should be able to begin a new term, a judge ruled Tuesday — despite a GOP election challenge centering on a pool of ballots that were cast but never counted.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: READ: Special counsel's final report in Trump Jan. 6 case
item.description: The Justice Department released special counsel Jack Smith's report on President-elect Donald Trump's election interference case, just days before he returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Trump's Greenland demands could impact Ozempic costs
item.description: President-elect Trump's threat to tariff Denmark if it resists his acquisition plans for the island territory of Greenland could disrupt one export that is wildly popular in America: Ozempic.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools brace for clash with immigration officials ahead of Trump term
item.description: K-12 schools across the U.S. are gearing up for potential efforts to shield their students as the incoming Trump administration sets its sights on deporting millions of people.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: RFK Jr. faces a complicated confirmation with some opposition on both sides of the aisle
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s views on vaccines and abortion rights are raising alarms across the political spectrum, as he's one of the more controversial picks for the president-elect's Cabinet.
item.dc:creator: Sarah McCammon, MPR News

item.title: Pete Hegseth heads to Capitol Hill for fiery hearing on his record, plans to shake up Pentagon
item.description: Pete Hegseth is set to take the hot seat before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday in a hearing that is sure to break out into fireworks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: An American tradition: Defeated candidates attending the president-elect's inauguration
item.description: In January 1981, Jimmy Carter nodded politely toward Ronald Reagan as the new Republican president thanked the Democrat for his administration's help after Reagan resoundingly defeated Carter the previous November.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams addresses migrants, crime and political future
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams had a wide-ranging press conference Monday discussing everything from closing down migrant shelters, to subway safety, to his tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: What Project 2025 says about the first 180 days of a presidency
item.description: Project 2025 lists four pillars as part of its plan, and the fourth pillar is an unpublished 180-day "Transition Playbook.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: People are rushing to install solar panels before Trump becomes President
item.description: Potential rooftop solar customers and installers worry the incoming Trump administration might try to eliminate a 30 percent federal tax credit. Some customers plan to install sooner because of that and solar companies are changing their business plans.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady, MPR News

item.title: Judge who blocked release of Trump report was 'plainly' wrong, special counsel tells appeals court
item.description: The Justice Department has asked a federal appeals court to move swiftly in reversing a judge’s order that had blocked the agency from releasing any part of special counsel Jack Smith’s investigative report on Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: ACLU sues Gov. DeSantis on behalf of Florida voters over special elections
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union is suing Gov. Ron DeSantis for not calling special elections for state House and Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Investigation clears Duluth mayor’s campaign manager and partner
item.description: An external investigation commissioned by the city of Duluth has found Mayor Roger Reinert’s partner and campaign manager Amber Gurske did not act improperly through her communications with city staff early in his administration.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump appears virtually in court to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is appearing virtually in a New York courtroom to be sentenced for his hush money conviction, 10 days before he is inaugurated.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump is sentenced in hush money case
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump received an unconditional discharge for his criminal conviction, meaning he will get a criminal record but no other penalties.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: U.S. stock markets close to honor former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: The New York Stock Exchange and the Nasdaq will both observe Thursday's national day of mourning in a Wall Street tradition dating back to 1865.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5
